sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.76 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: A semigroup presentation is called { tree-like} if all relations have the form$a=bc$where$a, b, c$are letters and no two relations share the left-hand side or the right-hand side. Is it decidable whether the semigroup given by a finite tree-like presentation contains an idempotent? This is equivalent to the question whether the closure of a finitely generated subgroup of R.\,Thompson's group$F$con...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
